mnemonics: DR. VECTOR: Data frames, Read functions, Vectors, Exploratory analysis, Coerce functions, Tibbles, Order functions, Reshape (for data manipulation concepts)
GALP: ggplot2, aes(), layers, position (for ggplot structure)
FACTOR Levels: Use factors for categorical data, not characters, to avoid incorrect analysis
keyPoints: R uses vectorized operations, which are more efficient than loops for data manipulation.
The tidyverse suite of packages (e.g., dplyr, ggplot2) provides a coherent and powerful framework for data analysis.
Understanding data types (numeric, character, factor, logical) and structures (vector, matrix, data frame, list) is fundamental to avoiding errors.
commonMistakes: Using `==` instead of `%in%` to check if elements belong to a set, leading to incorrect logical comparisons.
Forgetting to use `factor()` on character vectors representing categorical variables, which can cause problems in modeling and visualizations.
Using `$` on a tibble without ungrouping after a `dplyr::group_by()` operation, which can yield unexpected results.
